name: Golden Retriever
summary: Golden Retrievers go from light-coated, floppy puppies to athletic adults with a fuller feathered coat.
note: A profile puppy shot helps show paw size and loose ears before the adult frame fills in.
detailIntro: Golden Retrievers change visibly through the first two years. The ears stay soft, the coat begins lighter, and the body depth fills in before the adult feathering fully arrives.
puppyLook: Young Goldens often look all head, paws, and soft fluff.
adultLook: Adults carry a deeper chest, longer feathering, and a calmer expression.
photoAdvice: Use a side profile for the puppy photo so paw size and early proportions are easy to compare later.
Keep the adult shot at the same eye level and lens distance for a cleaner before-and-after pair.
Warm but neutral lighting helps coat color changes read clearly over time.

growthTimeline.title: Soft structure
growthTimeline.description: The body still reads compact and rounded, with a puppy coat that sits puffier than the final adult feathering.

growthTimeline.title: Leggy transition
growthTimeline.description: The frame lengthens quickly and the dog can look awkward for a while as paws, ears, and chest catch up at different speeds.

growthTimeline.title: Finished outline
growthTimeline.description: The chest deepens, feathering fills out, and the head shape settles into the mature Golden Retriever expression.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: Regular movement keeps this sporting breed balanced and helps the adult frame build evenly.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: Feathering develops more strongly with age, so coat maintenance matters more in adulthood.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: Goldens mature well when puppy enthusiasm is shaped into calm adult reliability.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: This breed usually thrives when the adult dog stays closely integrated with family routines.

name: German Shepherd
summary: The broad puppy face and low-set ears sharpen into the breed's upright silhouette and focused expression.
note: Use a front-facing puppy image to capture the ear transition before the adult profile becomes more angular.
detailIntro: German Shepherds change from broad, soft-featured puppies into strongly defined adults with upright ears, heavier chest structure, and a much more disciplined silhouette.
puppyLook: Puppies look softer, broader, and less finished through the ears and muzzle.
adultLook: Adults read sharper through the head, topline, and alert stance.
photoAdvice: Use a front or three-quarter puppy portrait to document the ear carriage transition clearly.
Keep the background simple so the dark saddle pattern and outline stay readable.
Try a standing adult pose to show how much more angular and deliberate the finished posture becomes.

growthTimeline.title: Broad puppy frame
growthTimeline.description: The head is softer, the coat can look plush, and the ear set may shift repeatedly during growth.

growthTimeline.title: Ear and limb transition
growthTimeline.description: The dog can look rangy as the limbs lengthen and the ears settle into their mature upright position.

growthTimeline.title: Adult focus
growthTimeline.description: The chest fills out, muscle develops, and the face takes on the more serious expression associated with the breed.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: Mental and physical tasks are both important once the dog moves past the puppy phase.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: The adult double coat drops heavily during seasonal changes.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: Good guidance matters early so drive matures into reliability rather than reactivity.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: Adult Shepherds do best when they have a role, routine, and close handler relationship.

name: French Bulldog
summary: Frenchies keep their signature ears, but their compact chest, jaw, and expression become far more defined as adults.
note: A close crop works well here because most of the visual change is in chest width, jaw shape, and stance.
detailIntro: French Bulldogs do not transform through height as dramatically as larger breeds, but the body depth, jaw shape, and mature stance change the look more than many owners expect.
puppyLook: The puppy face reads rounder, lighter, and more toy-like.
adultLook: Adults look denser through the chest and stronger through the jaw and shoulders.
photoAdvice: Keep both puppy and adult shots fairly tight because the facial structure carries most of the story.
Use the same seated or standing pose to make chest width easier to compare.
Avoid cluttered backgrounds that compete with the compact outline.

growthTimeline.title: Compact and rounded
growthTimeline.description: The puppy body is short, the face is extremely round, and the ears still dominate the overall look.

growthTimeline.title: Chest broadening
growthTimeline.description: The ribcage and shoulders become more obvious, giving the breed a sturdier shape.

growthTimeline.title: Mature expression
growthTimeline.description: The head, jaw, and stance settle, producing the adult Frenchie's compact but solid presence.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: Short, steady activity usually suits the breed better than sustained intensity.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: The short coat is simple, but skin folds and ears need routine attention.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: Consistency works better than repetition once puppy stubbornness appears.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: This breed usually thrives in close-contact household environments.

name: Siberian Husky
summary: Husky puppies start rounded and woolly, then lengthen into a leaner adult frame with a crisp facial mask.
note: Keep the background clean so the facial mask and eye shape stay readable between both stages.
detailIntro: Siberian Huskies start as plush, rounded puppies and then tighten into a lean working outline with much crisper facial markings and a more athletic line through the body.
puppyLook: Young Huskies look woollier, wider-eyed, and softer through the muzzle.
adultLook: Adults read lighter on their feet with a cleaner mask and more purposeful stance.
photoAdvice: Use uncluttered backgrounds so the facial mask remains the strongest visual feature.
Match coat season when possible because winter fluff can exaggerate the adult volume.
A standing profile helps show how much leaner the adult proportions become.

growthTimeline.title: Rounded fluff
growthTimeline.description: The puppy coat is thick and soft, often making the body look shorter and rounder than it really is.

growthTimeline.title: Lengthening frame
growthTimeline.description: Legs and body length become more apparent as the puppy fluff begins to settle.

growthTimeline.title: Lean adult finish
growthTimeline.description: The adult outline becomes much cleaner, with a crisp mask, balanced tail carriage, and athletic movement.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: This breed stays more manageable when adult energy has a daily outlet.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: The plush coat needs regular brushing, especially during heavy adult shed cycles.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: Early consistency matters because the adult dog can be clever and selective.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: A bored Husky often invents its own entertainment.

name: Dachshund
summary: The puppy version already hints at the long body, but the adult dog shows a more balanced chest and stronger topline.
note: Side-on photography is ideal because this breed's growth story is mostly about body proportions.
detailIntro: Dachshunds already show the long-backed silhouette as puppies, but the adult version carries noticeably better chest depth, body balance, and confidence through the stance.
puppyLook: The long body is visible early, but the chest and topline are still delicate.
adultLook: Adults look more grounded through the ribcage, shoulders, and overall stance.
photoAdvice: Use a clear side profile because this breed's story is mainly about proportion, not head shape.
Keep the dog on level ground so the topline does not get distorted.
Match posture in both stages for a cleaner comparison.

growthTimeline.title: Early silhouette
growthTimeline.description: Even as a tiny puppy, the body proportion already hints strongly at the adult breed outline.

growthTimeline.title: Body length settles
growthTimeline.description: The chest, shoulders, and neck start to balance out what initially looks like an exaggerated puppy frame.

growthTimeline.title: Finished ground-hugging stance
growthTimeline.description: The adult dog looks sturdier and more deliberate, with a stronger chest and more stable topline.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: Regular movement matters, but the breed benefits from protecting the back during growth and adulthood.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: Coat care is simple, though other varieties need more frequent attention.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: The bold personality matures best with calm repetition and clear boundaries.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: Dachshunds are small but opinionated, and the adult dog tends to stay very engaged with household activity.

name: Border Collie
summary: The fluffy puppy coat and oversized paws settle into a more aerodynamic adult outline built for movement.
note: Action-oriented adult images work especially well if the puppy image is calmer and closer framed.
detailIntro: Border Collies often begin as fluffy, soft-coated puppies and then mature into an athletic, fast-reading adult body built for movement and concentration.
puppyLook: Puppies look softer and looser, with oversized paws and a fluffier outline.
adultLook: Adults appear cleaner through the chest, neck, and movement line, even with a rough coat.
photoAdvice: A calm puppy portrait pairs well with a more dynamic adult stance to show the breed's working maturity.
Watch the coat direction in outdoor wind because it can distort the body line.
Use enough frame width to show posture, not just head detail.

growthTimeline.title: Soft-coated puppy
growthTimeline.description: The head looks rounder, the paws look oversized, and the posture still feels loose and playful.

growthTimeline.title: Adolescent stretch
growthTimeline.description: The frame lengthens and the dog begins to look more leggy and agile.

growthTimeline.title: Working outline
growthTimeline.description: The adult dog appears more aerodynamic, focused, and balanced through both head and body.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: Adult Border Collies usually need both movement and problem-solving work.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: The rough coat stays manageable with consistent upkeep.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: This breed matures best when intelligence is directed early and often.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: A settled adult Collie still tends to need structure and engagement every day.

name: Shiba Inu
summary: Shiba puppies have a teddy-bear roundness that tightens into the breed's fox-like adult face and curled posture.
note: A consistent seated pose highlights the change from rounded puppy cheeks to the cleaner adult head shape.
detailIntro: Shiba Inus often change from plush, teddy-like puppies into adults with a tighter fox-like face, more self-possessed posture, and a very characteristic curled tail carriage.
puppyLook: The puppy face is rounder, fuller, and almost stuffed-animal soft.
adultLook: Adults carry a cleaner wedge through the head and a more composed, upright posture.
photoAdvice: Keep both photos in a similar seated pose to show how the head shape refines over time.
Use simple backgrounds so the curled tail and body outline stay visible.
Consistent framing helps the shift from puppy roundness to adult sharpness stand out.

growthTimeline.title: Round puppy features
growthTimeline.description: The coat looks especially plush and the face feels much softer than the adult version.

growthTimeline.title: Head shape refines
growthTimeline.description: The muzzle and eye expression begin to sharpen while the puppy coat remains full.

growthTimeline.title: Fox-like finish
growthTimeline.description: The adult head, posture, and tail set define the breed much more clearly than in puppyhood.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: Shibas often do best with predictable activity rather than chaotic overhandling.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: The dense coat drops noticeably when the seasons turn.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: Short, clear training helps puppy stubbornness mature into manageable adult confidence.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: Many adults prefer order, space, and a steady routine.

name: Great Dane
summary: Great Dane puppies often look all legs, then mature into a tall, clean-lined adult with far more depth and presence.
note: Use a taller crop so the jump in height and leg length reads clearly from one stage to the next.
detailIntro: Great Danes may be the clearest example of dramatic growth. Puppies often look all legs and uncertainty, while adults become massive, balanced, and remarkably composed.
puppyLook: Puppies can seem oversized through the limbs and under-finished through the chest.
adultLook: Adults carry immense height, depth, and elegance once the frame catches up.
photoAdvice: Use a taller framing to make the jump in height visible instead of cropping too tightly.
Choose the same standing angle in both photos because proportion is the main story here.
Leave negative space above the adult dog so the final scale has room to read.

growthTimeline.title: All legs
growthTimeline.description: Early puppies often look oversized through the limbs, with a frame that has not yet balanced out.

growthTimeline.title: Rapid vertical growth
growthTimeline.description: Height arrives quickly and the body can look awkward as depth and substance catch up.

growthTimeline.title: Mass with balance
growthTimeline.description: The adult Dane becomes far more imposing, with added chest depth, topline stability, and a composed stance.

careProfile.label: Exercise
careProfile.description: Controlled movement matters more than frantic activity while the giant frame matures.

careProfile.label: Grooming
careProfile.description: The short coat is simple, but the breed's sheer size changes handling needs.

careProfile.label: Training
careProfile.description: A giant adult dog needs polished behavior long before full size arrives.

careProfile.label: Home life
careProfile.description: Even gentle adults take up real room in the home and in daily routines.
